ground_truth.title: 20 Best Gym Equipment Brands in 2025 (With Price Examples)
ground_truth.author: Aneta Niepytalska
ground_truth.main_content:
Do you want to set up a gym? Will you soon be buying equipment and are wondering what equipment is the best? Or maybe you dream of one or two pieces of exercise equipment for your home? In both cases, we have a long list of the best fitness brands in 2025 for you today. Read the article and compare price, quality and opinions in the fitness equipment industry to make the optimal choice.

Key takeaways

There are many good brands in the fitness equipment industry. Below we present some of them, both for professional and home use.
The prices of different pieces of equipment vary. One of the most expensive ones are Technogym’s treadmills: $9,999. The cheapest pieces of equipment are TRX’s suspension trainer tha costs $200.
If you want to start your gym, get familiar with the topic of gym management software. This tool will help you run your gym, save time and properly manage your customer base, employees, payments and attendance lists (thanks to a gym booking system).

20 Best Fitness Equipment Brands in 2025

1. Life Fitness 

Why Best? Innovative designs and durable machines make it a top choice for both commercial and home gyms.

Example Prices: Treadmill: $4,299 Elliptical: $3,199
Price Range: $2,000 – $10,000

2. Precor

Why Best? Advanced biomechanics and superior build quality ensure optimal performance and user comfort.

Example Prices: Treadmill: $5,799 Elliptical: $4,499
Price Range: $3,000 – $9,000

3. Cybex 

Why Best? Unique technologies like the Arc Trainer distinguish it from competitors, providing effective workouts.

Example Prices: Arc Trainer: $6,999 Treadmill: $7,999
Price Range: $2,500 – $10,000

4.Technogym 

Why Best? Integration with wellness apps and sleek, ergonomic designs cater to luxury markets and serious athletes.

Example Prices: Treadmill: $9,999, Exercise Bike: $2,499
Price Range: $2,500 – $15,000

5. Peloton

Why Best? Engaging live classes and high-quality equipment create a compelling fitness experience.

Example Prices: Bike+: $2,495, Tread: $3,495
Price Range: $2,000 – $4,500

6. Sole Fitness 

Why Best? Excellent customer reviews and solid warranties make it a trusted brand for home use.

Example Prices: Treadmill: $2,099, Elliptical: $1,799
Price Range: $800 – $3,000

7. Bowflex

Why Best? Products like the Max Trainer and SelectTech dumbbells offer effective, all-in-one workout solutions.

Example Prices: Max Trainer: $1,799, SelectTech Dumbbells: $399
Price Range: $400 – $2,500

8. NordicTrack

Why Best? Interactive iFit technology and robust machines provide immersive workout experiences.

Example Prices: Treadmill: $2,499, Bike: $1,999
Price Range: $1,000 – $4,000

9. Rogue Fitness

Why Best? Industry-leading durability and performance, favored by CrossFit and strength athletes.

Example Prices: Power Rack: $1,295, Barbell: $295
Price Range: $200 – $5,000+

10. Hammer Strength

Why Best? Legendary strength equipment designed for serious athletes and commercial gyms.

Example Prices: Leg Press: $4,999, Bench Press: $2,499
Price Range: $1,500 – $8,000

11. Matrix Fitness

Why Best? Cutting-edge technology and sleek designs appeal to modern fitness enthusiasts.

Example Prices: Treadmill: $5,999, Bike: $2,999
Price Range: $2,000 – $7,000

12. Inspire Fitness

Why Best? High-quality, versatile home gym systems that maximize space and functionality.

Example Prices: Home Gym: $2,999, Functional Trainer: $3,499
Price Range: $1,000 – $4,000

13. TRX

Why Best? Pioneered suspension training, offering portable and effective bodyweight workouts.

Example Prices: Suspension Trainer: $200, Force Kit: $350
Price Range: $100 – $500

14. Nautilus

Why Best? Trusted brand with a long history of producing reliable and effective fitness equipment.

Example Prices: Treadmill: $1,299, Bike: $899
Price Range: $500 – $2,500

15. ProForm

Why Best? Affordable yet feature-rich equipment, ideal for budget-conscious home users.

Example Prices: Treadmill: $999, Elliptical: $799
Price Range: $500 – $2,000

16. Schwinn Fitness

Why Best? Excellent value and durability, particularly known for high-quality exercise bikes.

Example Prices: Bike: $699, Rower: $899
Price Range: $400 – $1,500

17. Body-Solid

Why Best? Commercial-grade equipment at home gym prices, offering exceptional value.

Example Prices: Power Rack: $799, Bench: $299
Price Range: $200 – $2,000

18. Valor Fitness

Why Best? Budget-friendly strength equipment without compromising on quality and safety.

Example Prices: Power Rack: $499, Dumbbell Set: $299
Price Range: $100 – $1,500

19. Rep Fitness

Why Best? Rapidly growing brand known for high-quality, affordable strength equipment.

Example Prices: Power Rack: $699, Bench: $249
Price Range: $100 – $2,000

20. Titan Fitness

Why Best? Extremely affordable alternatives to premium brands, popular for home gyms.

Example Prices: Power Rack: $399, Barbell: $199
Price Range: $100 – $1,500
